Title: Genome-Wide Association Analyses Reveal the Genetic Basis of EMS Mutagenesis Efficiency in Rice

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: BKZH; HHZ (Rc); HHZ (rc); YHJZ; K9553; 420 diverse accessions
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antioxidant activity; scavenges free radicals; enhances seed survival under oxidative stress
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: proanthocyanidin biosynthetic pathway genes; flavonoid biosynthesis module
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates proanthocyanidin/flavonoid biosynthesis, leading to greater antioxidant capacity
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functional Rc alleles increase proanthocyanidin-associated antioxidant capacity, improve seed survival after EMS treatment, and increase retained genome-wide EMS-induced mutation frequency and visible mutant frequency
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; haplotype analysis; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; near-isogenic line comparison; whole-genome resequencing of M1 plants; M2 mutant frequency analysis; ROS/NBT staining; H2O2 measurement; CAT/SOD/POD enzyme assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS identified SRD7 on chromosome 7 as the major locus for EMS tolerance, with Rc as the strongest candidate gene. 2. Functional Rc haplotypes conferred smaller reductions in survival rate after EMS treatment than non-functional haplotypes. 3. CRISPR knockout of Rc in BKZH reduced seedling survival after EMS treatment. 4. Near-isogenic lines showed that functional Rc increased both seed survival and genome-wide EMS-induced SNP number as well as M2 visible mutant frequency. 5. Rc seeds accumulated less H2O2 and showed higher CAT, SOD, and POD activities after EMS treatment, supporting an antioxidant-based mechanism.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major regulator of proanthocyanidin biosynthesis and decisive determinant of seed antioxidant capacity underlying EMS tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: EMS-induced oxidative stress context; natural functional versus non-functional Rc alleles
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: seed/pericarp-associated based on pericarp pigmentation phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 0.6% EMS for 20 h; EMS mutagenesis-induced oxidative stress
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: functional Rc causes red pericarp pigmentation and is strongly linked to seed dormancy; may be undesirable in white-grained cultivars requiring rapid and uniform germination; also associated with pre-harvest sprouting resistance
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Functional Rc can be used to improve EMS mutagenesis efficiency and generate richer mutant allele pools; marker-assisted selection for functional haplotypes can prioritize parents for mutagenesis. However, pleiotropic effects on pericarp color and dormancy require balancing, and alternative strategies include engineering anthocyanin/flavonoid biosynthesis in elite white rice or using exogenous antioxidants.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper’s primary phenotype is EMS mutagenesis efficiency, not nutritional quality per se. Rc is included because it is the only core metabolism-related gene experimentally validated in Results and it regulates proanthocyanidin accumulation. The study proposes that Rc increases observed mutation load mainly by improving survival/retention of mutagenized embryonic cells rather than by increasing the initial mutation rate. Functional haplotypes H1, H2, and H4 are active, whereas H3 and H5 are non-functional due to a 14-bp deletion in exon 6; H2 is Aus-specific. Rc is also noted in Discussion to regulate ABA biosynthesis in prior literature, but that was not experimentally tested here.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CRISPR/Cas9 sgRNAs driven by OsU6a and OsU6b promoters (inducible/constitutive classification not stated)
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing of M1 mutant pools and NIL-derived M1 plants; accession numbers NA
